# Zig

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by .github/scripts/gen_language_docs.py - DO NOT EDIT.
     Edit the source matrix JSON / workflow and re-run the generator instead. -->

How `json2vars-setter` handles Zig, and how the example workflow provisions it.

## json2vars-setter integration

| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Matrix JSON key | `versions.zig` |
| Action output | `versions_zig` |
| Dynamic-update strategy input | `zig-strategy` (`stable` / `latest` / `both`) |
| Version source | see [Version Sources](../reference/version-sources.md) |

## Supported runners

The example matrix runs on **Ubuntu / Windows / macos-15**:

`ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, `macos-15`

Any GitHub-hosted runner the setup action supports also works - the OS list is just the `os` array in your matrix JSON.

## Supported versions

Example matrix (what the example workflow runs):

```json
{
  "versions": {
    "zig": [
      "0.14.1",
      "0.15.2"
    ]
  }
}
```

Latest values tracked by the dynamic-update tooling (auto-refreshed from the release APIs into the version cache):

| Field | Version |
| --- | --- |
| `stable` | `0.14.1` |
| `latest` | `0.15.2` |

_Cache last updated: 2026-06-24T12:28:41.970710._

## Setup action (example workflow)

[`zig_test.yml`](https://github.com/7rikazhexde/json2vars-setter/blob/main/.github/workflows/zig_test.yml) provisions Zig with the matrix value json2vars-setter produced:

```yaml
- name: Set up Zig
  uses: mlugg/setup-zig@v2.2.1
  with:
    version: ${{ matrix.zig-version }}
```
